anchor: We’re scaling fast and need a senior DevOps & Cloud Engineer (5+ years) to hit the ground running—AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, Docker, and CI/CD with GitHub Actions or Jenkins, plus some on‑call experience. Ideally you’ve built reliable pipelines in a fast‑moving startup, understand blue‑green deployments, and know how to secure infra with IAM, KMS, and VPC flow logs. We’re open to AWS certified folks and offer a remote‑friendly, full‑time role from any U.S. time zone—job starts ASAP. If you thrive in a collaborative, low‑latency environment and love pulling numbers to prove performance gains, let’s chat!

positive.summary: Senior DevOps & Cloud Engineer with 8.5 years of experience building resilient CI/CD pipelines, secure AWS infrastructures, and blue‑green deployments for fast‑moving tech startups.

work_experience.description: Designed and maintained CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions and Jenkins, automated Kubernetes deployments, implemented blue‑green strategies, and managed on‑call rotation to ensure 99.9% uptime for the ride‑hailing platform.

Lead the migration of legacy monolith to microservices architecture on AWS, built scalable CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions, managed Terraform infra code, enforced IAM policies, and introduced blue‑green deployment workflows across multiple services. Coordinated 24/7 on‑call shifts and reduced deployment latency by 35%.

projects.name: BlueGreenDeploy
projects.description: Automated blue‑green deployment pipeline for Kubernetes services using Helm and Argo CD, reducing downtime by 90%.

projects.name: InfraCAS
projects.description: Infrastructure as Code stack for multi‑region AWS deployment, including IAM, KMS, VPC, and CloudWatch logging.

projects.name: PerformancePulse
projects.description: Real‑time monitoring dashboard aggregating Prometheus metrics across services, with alerting for SLA compliance.

Lead the mobile engineering team to deliver the Careem rider and driver apps, integrating GraphQL, REST, and WebSocket services. Implemented OTA updates, push notifications, and a robust CI/CD pipeline using Fastlane, Bitrise, and GitHub Actions. projects.description: Cross‑platform restaurant discovery app featuring real‑time reviews and adaptive UI.

anchor:
- Backend Engineer (Go, Rust, Python, Django) – Full‑time, on‑site at HQ (Austin, TX) or remote (US‑wide)  
- 5+ years of production‑level backend development, with strong experience in microservices, API design, and gRPC  
- Proficient in Go and/or Rust for performance‑critical services; Python + Django for rapid iteration and web apps  
- Demonstrated expertise building scalable data pipelines, distributed caching, and integration with message queues (Kafka, RabbitMQ, NSQ)  
- Familiarity with SQL (PostgreSQL) and NoSQL (Cassandra, DynamoDB, Redis) databases, including schema design and query tuning  
- Solid understanding of DevOps practices: Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, ArgoCD)  
- Experience with cloud environments (AWS or GCP) – VPCs, IAM, Autoscaling, Cloud Functions, CloudWatch/Stackdriver monitoring  
- Strong grasp of distributed system concepts: eventual consistency, CAP theorem, sharding, and partition tolerance  
- Proven ability to write clean, well‑tested code (unit + integration tests) and maintain clear documentation  
- Excellent problem‑solving skills, low‑latency performance tuning, and a track record of reducing system downtime  
- Deep knowledge of RESTful design, GraphQL, or protobuf/JSON API standards; Swagger/OpenAPI/GraphQL schema experience preferred  
- Comfortable with version control (Git), code review, and continuous integration pipelines  
- Willingness to travel up to 25% on a quarterly basis for cross‑team collaboration and quarterly off‑site hackathons  
- Must hold a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (Master’s preferred)  
- Security‑clearance eligibility or SB‑1 background check acceptable for future compliance roles  
- Availability to start in 6–8 weeks (on‑site relocation package available).

work_experience.description: Designed and maintained microservices architecture in Go for music streaming platform, leveraging gRPC for inter-service communication, messaging with Kafka, and high-availability with Kubernetes. Tuned PostgreSQL and Redis for performance.

Developed RESTful and GraphQL APIs using Python and Django for e-commerce platform, integrated with PostgreSQL, Cassandra, and DynamoDB, and published services on AWS. Implemented CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and Docker, automated deployments with Helm and Kubernetes.

Led design and implementation of high-throughput streaming microservices in Go and Rust, built gRPC APIs, integrated with Kafka and RabbitMQ, and implemented distributed caching with Redis. Managed AWS infrastructure (VPC, IAM, Autoscaling, CloudWatch) and orchestrated services using Docker, Kubernetes, and Helm. Developed CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and ArgoCD.

work_experience.description: Designed and implemented a unified observability stack (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, OpenTelemetry) to monitor 50+ microservices. Orchestrated zero‑downtime deployments on Kubernetes using Helm. Directed incident response with PagerDuty, Slack, and JIRA, reducing MTTR by 40%. Developed Terraform modules for AWS infrastructure.

Led the migration of core services to GCP, implementing Kubernetes clusters and a comprehensive EFK stack. Automated infrastructure provisioning with Terraform and GCP Deployment Manager. Established observability culture, integrating distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry and Jaeger. Managed incident response processes, achieving a 30% reduction in incident severity.

Architecting and scaling a globally distributed e‑commerce platform to support 100M+ monthly active users with zero‑downtime deployments. Implementing automated rollout pipelines with ArgoCD and Helm, monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana, and log aggregation via EFK. Lead incident post‑mortems and continuous improvement initiatives.

anchor:
Senior Frontend Engineer – React 18 (v18.3.0), Next.js 13.4 (App Router), TypeScript 5.1, Tailwind CSS 3.3.1

We’re building a server‑first, edge‑enabled web app and need a senior developer with:
- 5 + years of production front‑end experience (React/Next.js)  
- Deep knowledge of React Query v5 (or SWR) for data‑fetching, and experience with *app router* edge API routes  
- Comfortable with modern React patterns (hooks, context, Zustand/Redux Toolkit) and TypeScript 5.1 typings  
- Proven ability to design and ship a component library using Tailwind v3.3 + CSS Modules or styled‑components, with automated style linting (prettier, eslint‑plugin-tailwindcss)  
- Hands‑on experience deploying to Vercel (Netlify/GCP) in a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab) with Dockerizing for Serverless functions  
- Unit tests (Jest v29 + React Testing Library) and E2E Cypress v13 test suite, with BDD using Cucumber integration preferred  
- Optional: AWS Lambda, S3, CloudFront expertise and familiarity with App Router’s middleware  
- Clear writing of accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) and responsive design principles  

We’re headquartered in Seattle but offer full‑time remote work across US time zones. Candidates must be US residents, able to start within 2 weeks, and able to sign a non‑disclosure agreement. Ideal applicants will bring startup‑scale agility and a strong portfolio (GitHub/GitLab) of past Next.js + Tailwind projects.

positive.summary: Senior Frontend Engineer with 6+ years of production‑grade experience building web applications using React 18, Next.js 13.4 (App Router), and TypeScript 5.1. Skilled in creating component libraries with Tailwind 3.3, deploying edge‑enabled serverless APIs to Vercel, and implementing comprehensive testing suites with Jest, Cypress, and Cucumber. US resident ready to start within 2 weeks.

work_experience.description: Developed patient portal interface using React 17, Next.js 12, and TypeScript. Created reusable component library with Tailwind 3.0 and CSS Modules. Deployed to Netlify with a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Wrote unit tests with Jest and integrated end‑to‑end tests with Cypress.

Led front‑end engineering for Careem’s global mobile portal, delivering a scalable component library built with React 18, Next.js 13.4 App Router, TypeScript 5.1, and Tailwind 3.3. Implemented serverless edge APIs, React Query v5 data fetching, and comprehensive state management with Zustand and Redux Toolkit. Established CI/CD pipelines in GitHub Actions, Dockerized serverless functions, and deployed to Vercel. Authored unit tests with Jest and RTL, and E2E suites with Cypress 13 and BDD integration via Cucumber. Ensured WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility and responsive design across devices.

projects.name: EdgeUI
projects.description: Component library for edge‑enabled web apps, built with React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind 3.3, and styled‑components. Includes automated style linting and comprehensive test suites.

projects.name: DrugFinder
projects.description: E‑commerce platform for pharmaceuticals using Next.js 13, React Query v5, SWR, and Zustand for state management.

projects.name: HealthPortal
projects.description: Patient portal for Vezeeta, featuring responsive UI, accessibility compliance, and end‑to‑end testing.

work_experience.description: Assisted senior testers in vulnerability assessments, web application pentests, and IAM configuration reviews.
Conducted red‑team exercises and exploited zero‑day vulnerabilities in web applications. Implemented SOC blue‑print workshops.
Lead pentest engagements for multi‑tenant SaaS, performed OWASP top‑10 assessments, and designed IAM hardening blueprints for AWS and Azure AD.

projects.name: ZeroDay Exploit Framework (ZDEF)
projects.description: Custom framework using Python and Metasploit to identify and exploit zero‑day CVEs in SaaS.

projects.name: IAM Hardening Automation
projects.description: Automated audit of AWS IAM policies and Azure AD permissions to enforce least privilege.

projects.name: SOC Blue‑Print Workshop Toolkit
projects.description: Toolkit to guide SOC teams in creating blue‑print response playbooks.

certifications: Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate

negative.summary: Mid-level Frontend Engineer with 4.5 years of professional experience in React and Next.js, specializing in component libraries, SSR, legacy browser support, and accessibility.

work_experience.description: Built patient booking UI with React 16, integrated Redux Toolkit, created reusable components with Emotion and SCSS modules, ensured IE11 compatibility via polyfills, wrote unit tests with Jest and RTL, and documented components in Storybook. Deployed to Netlify using GitHub Actions.

Led the design and implementation of Careem’s company-wide component library using React 17, Next.js 12, and Emotion. Implemented SSR with getServerSideProps, added IE11 polyfills, wrote comprehensive accessibility layers (WCAG 2.0 AA), and achieved 80% code quality with fast debugging. Deployed via Netlify with GitHub Actions, Dockerised dev environments, and added Playwright for E2E testing.

projects.name: Next.js Component Library
projects.description: Designed a reusable component library with Emotion, SCSS modules, and Storybook, supporting SSR and IE11 fallback, deployed on Netlify.

projects.name: E2E Testing Suite
projects.description: Developed Playwright-based E2E tests for a multi‑tenant web app, covering accessibility and performance metrics.

projects.name: Legacy Browser Polyfill Integration
projects.description: Implemented polyfills for IE11 in a complex Next.js application, ensuring full functionality across legacy browsers.
